$comment: Windy DROPS ops surface — Class C. Dev-name/brand split: the consumer brand is 'Windy Drops', but the deployed service (and /version's service field, and the fleet-version key) is 'windy-registry' — so product=windy-registry. Its source lives in the windy-registry repo, separate from the windy-drops repo (which is the SDK + artifact-spec + CLI). Clean single-service replication. Product API (browse/publish/install drops, library, ratings, Stripe payouts, federation) stays OUT per §2. Live at https://api.windydrops.com. get_capabilities → /health/full, which actively probes Postgres + R2 + both JWKS (Pro + Eternitas) — a genuinely deep readiness probe. RESOLVED 2026-07-13: the live 'r2_bucket: http 404' flagged during the retrofit was a FALSE ALARM — the old probe HEAD'd the public-domain root, which an R2 public bucket 404s BY DESIGN (bundles downloaded fine and sha256-verified throughout). The probe now HEADs the newest REAL published bundle (root-404 = 'ok (empty)' when nothing is published yet), and r2 participates in the overall ok/degraded verdict. LIVE only after the Grant-gated image rebuild.

get_status.note: MF1 deploy identity: service, version, commit sha, started_at, environment.

get_capabilities.note: actively probes Postgres + R2 bucket + JWKS (pro + eternitas); the real readiness/capability probe — never 500s, every dependency maps to a string.

run_selftest.note: canary: resolve + fetch a known drop end to end.

restart_app.note: out-of-band; ops plane outside the patient.

check_for_update.note: wire to the Steamroller fleet version manifest (L2).

reset_to_defaults.note: NEVER — holds the drops catalog + creator payouts.

tools.name: get_health
tools.description: The FIRST call when Windy Drops seems down. Liveness of the drops registry: returns ok if the API process is up and answering.

tools.name: get_status
tools.description: Read Windy Drops' deployment identity — which registry build is serving right now, its commit, when it started, and which environment. Use this to confirm a deploy actually landed.

tools.name: get_capabilities
tools.description: Deep readiness of Windy Drops: actively checks whether the database, the R2 bundle storage, and both trust-key sources (Windy Pro and Eternitas) are reachable. Use this when drops won't publish, download, or verify — it names exactly which dependency is broken.
